Container.Name: reminders-postgres
Container.Overview: Postgres 16 backing the Accountability goal tracker. Pairs with the reminders-app container.
Container.Requires: Create a custom Docker network named "reminders" first: open a console and run `docker network create reminders`. Both this container and reminders-app must join it so they can reach each other by container name.
Container.Description:
Postgres 16 dedicated to the Accountability app.
Before adding: create the shared Docker network the app and DB use to talk to each other. From the Unraid terminal:
docker network create reminders
The reminders-app container's DATABASE_URL points at this container by name (reminders-postgres). No host port is published by default — only the app needs to reach it, and it does so over the internal network.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Where Postgres stores its data on disk. Back this up with the CA Backup/Restore plugin.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_USER
Config.Description: Database username. Must match the user in the app's DATABASE_URL.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Database password. Must match the password in the app's DATABASE_URL. Use a long random string.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_DB
Config.Description: Database name. Must match the database in the app's DATABASE_URL.

Config.Name: Postgres port (LAN)
Config.Description: OPTIONAL. Leave blank to keep Postgres internal to Docker. Only publish if you want to connect with an external client like pgAdmin.
